name: Dubai
culturalNote: Modest dress is appreciated in malls, souks, and public spaces. Swimwear is fine at beaches and pools.

name: Riyadh
culturalNote: Dress modestly in public. Women no longer need to wear abaya by law, but modest clothing is respectful.

name: Jeddah
culturalNote: More relaxed than Riyadh — modest Western wear is widely accepted, especially near the Corniche.

name: Abu Dhabi
culturalNote: Cover shoulders and knees when visiting mosques or government buildings. Beachwear is appropriate at resorts only.

name: Doha
culturalNote: Modest dress in public spaces, souqs, and government buildings. Malls and beach resorts are more relaxed.

name: Kuwait City
culturalNote: Conservative dress in public is expected. Avoid very short or sleeveless clothing in public areas.

name: Muscat
culturalNote: Omani culture is welcoming but modest dress shows respect. Cover arms and knees in souqs and mosques.

name: Cairo
culturalNote: Lightweight, modest clothing works best. Women are more comfortable with covered shoulders in older districts.

name: Amman
culturalNote: Modern and relatively relaxed. Modest dress is respectful in older parts of the city.

name: Beirut
culturalNote: One of the most fashion-forward cities in the Middle East — Western styles are the norm in most areas.

name: Casablanca
culturalNote: International city with a relaxed dress code. Modest dress is appreciated in the medina and traditional areas.

name: Marrakech
culturalNote: Cover shoulders and knees in the medina. The riads and souqs require respectful, modest dressing.

name: London
culturalNote: No specific cultural dress requirements. Smart casual is the standard for most social occasions.

name: Paris
culturalNote: Parisians tend towards understated elegance — quality fabrics and clean cuts over logo-heavy fashion.

name: Rome
culturalNote: Cover shoulders and knees when visiting churches. Romans dress stylishly for all occasions.

name: Barcelona
culturalNote: Relaxed and fashion-forward. Cover up for religious sites. Beach-to-bar outfits are common near La Barceloneta.

name: Amsterdam
culturalNote: Very casual and practical city — locals are comfortable over stylish, but smart casual fits any occasion.

name: Berlin
culturalNote: Berlin is famously individual — creative, edgy fashion is celebrated. Practicality is valued over formality.

name: Istanbul
culturalNote: Cover shoulders and knees for mosque visits. Istanbul blends secular and traditional styles seamlessly.

name: Athens
culturalNote: Cover up for Orthodox churches. Lightweight fabrics are essential for the hot, dry summers.

name: Milan
culturalNote: The fashion capital of Italy — dress smartly at all times, especially near the Quadrilatero della Moda.

name: Madrid
culturalNote: Smart casual for evenings. Madrileños dress up for dinner — casual beachwear is not appropriate in the city centre.

name: Lisbon
culturalNote: Relaxed and welcoming. Smart casual works for most occasions. Comfortable shoes are essential on the hilly cobblestone streets.

name: Vienna
culturalNote: Vienna has a formal Central European dress culture — smart casual or above is expected for concerts, museums, and restaurants.

name: Stockholm
culturalNote: Swedes are practical and stylish — functional yet fashionable is the Swedish standard. Waterproofing is important year-round.

name: Prague
culturalNote: Central European city with a relaxed dress culture. Smart casual is appropriate for most occasions.

name: New York
culturalNote: New York has no dress code — but locals dress sharp. Layering is essential due to aggressive air conditioning indoors.

name: Los Angeles
culturalNote: LA is casual and sun-soaked. Even smart casual events lean towards relaxed elegance.

name: Miami
culturalNote: Miami is one of the most fashion-conscious cities in the US — bold colours and confident styles are celebrated.

name: Chicago
culturalNote: Chicagoans are practical dressers — wind and cold demand layering. Smart casual is the city standard.

name: Toronto
culturalNote: Practical and polished — layering is essential year-round due to variable temperatures.

name: Mexico City
culturalNote: Relatively relaxed dress culture. Smart casual is appropriate for most occasions. The city is at high altitude — pack a layer even in summer.

name: Tokyo
culturalNote: Japanese fashion culture is highly diverse. Removing shoes when entering traditional spaces is key. Conservative and polished dress is appreciated.

name: Singapore
culturalNote: Lightweight fabrics are a must year-round. Smart casual is standard. Cover up for temples and mosques.

name: Bangkok
culturalNote: Cover shoulders and knees for temple visits. Light, breathable fabrics are essential in the heat.

name: Sydney
culturalNote: Sydney is casual and outdoor-focused. Smart casual is appropriate for most events. Note: seasons are reversed — December is summer.

name: Melbourne
culturalNote: Melbourne is Australia's fashion capital — polished and creative. Famously unpredictable weather means layers are essential. Seasons are reversed.

name: Seoul
culturalNote: South Koreans are highly fashion-conscious — polished, trendy outfits are the norm, especially in Gangnam.

name: Hong Kong
culturalNote: Extremely fashion-conscious city. Air conditioning indoors is very aggressive — always carry a light layer.

name: Taipei
culturalNote: Casual and modern city. Lightweight fabrics for the humid summers. Temples require modest, respectful dress.

name: Mumbai
culturalNote: Respectful, modest dress is appreciated. Monsoon season (June–Sept) requires practical, quick-dry fabrics.

name: Kuala Lumpur
culturalNote: Multi-cultural city — cover shoulders for mosques. Light, breathable fabrics are essential year-round.

name: Bali
culturalNote: A sarong and sash are required for temple visits. Resort-wear and bohemian styles are widely accepted.

name: Jakarta
culturalNote: Modest dress is respectful in this large Muslim-majority city. Lightweight, breathable fabrics are essential year-round.

name: Lagos
culturalNote: Lagos has a vibrant and fashion-forward culture. Smart casual to smart dress is common for most occasions. Modest dress for religious sites.

name: Nairobi
culturalNote: Nairobi sits at high altitude, making it surprisingly cool. A light jacket is useful year-round, even in summer. Modest dress for most occasions.

name: Cape Town
culturalNote: Casual and relaxed city. Smart casual works for most occasions. Note: seasons are reversed — December is summer.

name: São Paulo
culturalNote: Brazil's fashion capital — Paulistanos are style-conscious. Note: seasons are reversed — December is summer.

name: Buenos Aires
culturalNote: Porteños (Buenos Aires residents) are famously elegant and stylish. Smart casual is the minimum expected. Seasons are reversed.
